description:
Make tagging fast and low-friction.

Improvements:
- Inline champion tag editor (no scroll reset)
- Tagging queue mode for batch processing
- Autocomplete expression builder
- Fast tag filtering and search

Done when: Tagging 20+ champions is frictionless. No context loss when editing tags.
title: Phase 5: Tagging UX Improvements

description:
Enable customizable truth layers.

Scopes: self, team, global. Overlay resolution: self overrides team overrides global.

Implementation:
- Add self scope for tags, requirements, compositions
- Implement effective overlay resolution logic
- Clearly indicate editing scope in UI
- Enable promotion flow (self -> team -> global)

Done when: Users can customize locally without affecting global truth. Teams can maintain shared definitions. Overlay logic is deterministic and tested.
title: Phase 4: Scope & Overlay System

description: Review existing styles.css and inline styles in app.js for hardcoded color values (#cc5b34, #f5f2e9, #9c3f1e, etc.) and replace with var() references from variables.css. This is an ongoing cleanup task — tackle incrementally as areas are touched during other work. Track progress by file/section.
title: USS: Migrate existing hardcoded colors to CSS variable references

description:
Users need the ability to reset their password if forgotten.

Scope:
- Backend: password reset flow (request reset token, validate token, set new password)
- Frontend: reset request form and new password form
- Email delivery for reset link (or token display if no email service configured)

Considerations:
- Tokens should be time-limited and single-use
- Should integrate with existing auth system in server/auth/
title: Add password reset for registered accounts

description: Define the USS pill component pattern. Review existing pill usage (active filter pills, tag pills, scope pills) and unify into a single reusable .uss-pill class with variants (default, active, removable). Document in docs/USS.md. Migrate existing pill instances to use the standardized classes and variables from variables.css.
title: USS: Standardize Pill component

close_reason: Implemented: draft-context endpoint, listMembersWithPools, pool snapshot with Role(Player) display, merged Review into Composer layout. Pushed in commit 5097417.
description:
Make Composer truthful and structurally correct.

Backend:
- Create endpoint to fetch resolved team draft context (roster + role assignments + role-specific pools)
- Ensure role -> user -> pool mapping is deterministic and correct

Frontend:
- Fix Pool Snapshot so each role reflects the correct teammate champion pool
- Normalize role display: Role (PlayerName) for all roles
- Remove artificial gating requiring champion selection to unlock review
- Merge Review functionality directly into Composer layout

Done when: Selecting a team always produces role-correct pools. Composer no longer requires fake input to proceed. Composer clearly represents constraints -> checks -> tree flow.
title: Phase 1: Composer Correctness & Team Context

description: Define the USS list component pattern. Review existing list usage (summary-card-list, coming-soon-list, roster lists, tree node lists) and unify into reusable list classes with consistent spacing, borders, and item styling. Document in docs/USS.md. Migrate existing list instances to standardized classes and variables.
title: USS: Standardize List component

description:
Make DraftEngine feel cohesive.

Improvements:
- Standardized page layout patterns
- Reduced instructional copy density
- Composer as 2-panel layout (Setup + Evaluation/Tree)
- Unified toast/status feedback system

Done when: Navigation is intuitive. Composer is the clear core workflow.
title: Phase 6: UI & Workflow Polish

description: Define the USS filter component pattern. Review existing filter usage (explorer filters, draft picks modal filters, tag catalog) and unify into reusable filter classes with consistent label styling, clear buttons, collapsible sections, and dropdown panels. Document in docs/USS.md. Migrate existing filter instances to standardized classes and variables.
title: USS: Standardize Filter component

description: Define the USS default for label styling within bordered context sections (like builder-context-row). Currently ad-hoc — needs a codified pattern covering font-size, weight, color, and alignment rules so all context sections are uniform. Includes app-wide audit to identify and migrate existing mismatched labels.
title: USS: Define context-section label pattern

description:
Ship a stable v1.0.

Stability:
- Tests for overlay resolution, clause evaluation, pruning correctness
- Stable seed scripts and migrations

Onboarding Flow:
1. Create account
2. Create team
3. Assign roles
4. Configure pools
5. Select composition
6. Run composer

Done when: Full workflow works without Riot integration. System is stable, explainable, and demo-ready.
title: Phase 7: v1.0 Hardening

description: Audit the full UI for component patterns not yet covered by USS. Candidates include: cards, form inputs, dropdowns, tooltips, badges, tabs, panels, toolbars, and any other repeated visual patterns. For each, document current usage locations, variations, and propose a unified USS class. This is an ongoing discovery task — update as new patterns are found during development.
title: USS: Component audit — identify remaining patterns for standardization

description:
Replace toggle-based checks with composable tag clauses.

Requirement Model - each Requirement has one or more TagClauses with: expr (AND/OR/NOT + parentheses), minCount, optional maxCount, optional roleFilter.

Evaluation Engine:
- Evaluate each clause against current lineup
- Return per-clause pass/fail with explanation
- Prepare for tree pruning via currentMatches, remainingSlots, maxPossibleMatches

Done when: Requirements are fully data-driven. Evaluation output is structured and explainable. No hardcoded composition booleans remain.
title: Phase 2: Tag-Driven Requirements System

description:
Bundle Requirements into named Composition profiles.

Composition Model: name, description, list of requirementIds, scope (self/team/global).

Composer Integration:
- Select active Composition
- Display per-requirement evaluation
- Tree pruning eliminates impossible branches

Done when: Multiple compositions can exist. Composer evaluates based on selected composition. Valid leaves can be filtered by composition success.
title: Phase 3: Compositions (Requirement Bundles)

close_reason: Fixed: changed payload.draftContext.teamId === teamId to === Number(teamId) in fetchBuilderDraftContext in public/app/app.js
description:
fetchBuilderDraftContext compares payload.draftContext.teamId (number from parsePositiveInteger) with state.builder.teamId (string from select/teamConfig). Strict === always fails, so draftContext stays null and pool snapshot falls back to profile pools — showing only the logged-in user's own champion pools instead of all team members'.

Fix: change comparison to Number(teamId) so the response is accepted.
title: Fix draft context silently discarded due to teamId type mismatch

description: Create a dedicated CSS variables file (variables.css) that centralizes all USS design tokens. Move existing inline var() definitions (--bg, --surface, --accent, --warn, etc.) into this file. All new components must reference these variables. This enables single-point theme changes instead of agents crawling code. Include color tokens, spacing, border-radius, font stacks, and z-index layers.
title: USS: Create variables.css with design tokens
